key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: Mid-cycle operating income proxy $2040.5M ÷ 658.621M shares; FY2025 OCF $3099.0M and capex $3779.0M per AAL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260218_rpt20251231_acc0000006201_26_000014.htm
Mid-cycle demand normalization; modest deleveraging from OCF; exit 7× owner cash

open_questions: Confirm mid-cycle operating income normalization versus OCF proxy (**$4.70/sh**).
AAdvantage co-brand milestone band (**$0–$20/sh**) pending standalone disclosure.
Live price and final base IRR after mechanical refresh.
thesis: American Airlines Group is the holding company for American Airlines, a major U.S. network carrier operating a hub-and-spoke domestic and international passenger and cargo network. **[Fact]** Shares trade on Nasdaq (AAL). **[Fact]** FY2025 total operating revenue was **$54.6 billion**, operating income **$1.5 billion**, and net income **$111 million** (diluted earnings **$0.17** per share) per `investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260218_rpt20251231_acc0000006201_26_000014.htm`. **[Fact]** FY2025 cash from operations was **$3.1 billion** while capital spending was **$3.8 billion**, and total debt and finance lease obligations were approximately **$29.0 billion** against cash of **$902 million** at year-end 2025.
why_market_wrong: The stock may trade as a distressed equity stub on a highly levered network, while normalized demand and loyalty economics still generate substantial operating cash flow. **[Inference]** FY2025 operating income fell to **$1.5 billion** from **$2.6 billion** in FY2024, so generalists may extrapolate margin compression without crediting ongoing debt paydown from **$3.1 billion** of operating cash flow. **[Inference]** The AAdvantage loyalty program and co-brand partnerships produce high-margin cash that is not separately capitalized on the balance sheet; the market may overweight GAAP net income (**$111 million**) versus mid-cycle owner cash per share near **$4.70** from operating cash flow. **[Inference]** Proof-first component math at base case sums to about **−$9.79** per share of filing-locked economic value versus a price near **$15**, suggesting the market prices deleveraging and cycle recovery beyond what the conservative component schedule captures today.
